2026-04-17T14:59:41.2346374Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T14:59:41.2346812Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T14:59:41.2347195Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T14:59:41.2347514Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T14:59:41.2347757Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T14:59:41.2347924Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-17T14:59:41.2348114Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-17T14:59:41.2348544Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-17T14:59:41.2349057Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-17T14:59:41.2349322Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"},"name":"mention_owners"}]}}}

2026-04-17T15:04:00.9534395Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T15:00:49.803942615Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58292,\"title\":\"[CognitiveServices] Docs: Document new resource types added in v1.6.0-beta.1 (2026-01-15-preview)\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57510 (commit `8ba2ed962`) by `@azure-sdk-bot`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57510 upgraded `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices` to version 1.6.0-beta.1 targeting the `2026-01-15-preview` API version. This introduced several new resource types to the generated SDK:\\n\\n- `CognitiveServicesAgentApplication` — manages agent applications under a Cognitive Services Project (`Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts/projects/applications`)\\n- `CognitiveServicesAgentDeployment` — manages agent deployments under a Cognitive Services Project\\n- `RaiExternalSafetyProviderSchema` — manages external safety provider schemas for Responsible AI\\n- `RaiToolLabel` — manages RAI tool labels\\n- `RaiTopic` — manages RAI topics\\n- `SubscriptionRaiPolicy` — manages subscription-scoped RAI policies\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The CHANGELOG entry for `1.6.0-beta.1` only states _\\u0026#34;Upgraded api-version to 2026-01-15-preview\\u0026#34;_ — it does not enumerate the new resource types or capabilities added by this API version bump\\n- The README **Key concepts** section has no content specific to this package; it just links to generic Azure SDK guidelines — new resource categories (Agent resources, RAI resources) are not described\\n- The README **Examples** section contains no code samples at all, just a link to a generic samples gallery; none of the resource types (including the newly added ones) have usage examples\\n- The `samples/` or `tests/Samples/` directory has no sample files, meaning there are no test-backed snippets to be injected into the README\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Update the CHANGELOG\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/CHANGELOG.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — expand the `1.6.0-beta.1` entry to enumerate new resource types.\\n\\nReplace the current `1.6.0-beta.1` section:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.6.0-beta.1 (2026-04-17)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Upgraded api-version to 2026-01-15-preview.\\n```\\n\\nWith the following expanded entry:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.6.0-beta.1 (2026-04-17)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Upgraded api-version to 2026-01-15-preview.\\n- Added `CognitiveServicesAgentApplicationResource` and `CognitiveServicesAgentApplicationCollection` to manage agent applications under a Cognitive Services Project (`Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts/projects/applications`).\\n- Added `CognitiveServicesAgentDeploymentResource` and `CognitiveServicesAgentDeploymentCollection` to manage agent deployments under a Cognitive Services Project.\\n- Added `RaiExternalSafetyProviderSchemaResource` and `RaiExternalSafetyProviderSchemaCollection` for managing external safety provider schemas.\\n- Added `RaiToolLabelResource` and `RaiToolLabelCollection` for managing RAI tool labels.\\n- Added `RaiTopicResource` and `RaiTopicCollection` for managing RAI topics.\\n- Added `SubscriptionRaiPolicyResource` and `SubscriptionRaiPolicyCollection` for subscription-scoped RAI policy management.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Update the README Key concepts section\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/README.md`\\n**Action:** Edit — replace the generic Key concepts section with package-specific content.\\n\\nReplace:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nKey concepts of the Microsoft Azure SDK for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\nWith:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThis library provides resource management for Microsoft Azure Cognitive Services. Key resource types include:\\n\\n- **CognitiveServicesAccountResource** — top-level Cognitive Services account\\n- **CognitiveServicesAccountDeploymentResource** — model deployments within an account\\n- **CognitiveServicesProjectResource** — AI projects within an account\\n- **CognitiveServicesAgentApplicationResource** — agent applications within a project (introduced in `2026-01-15-preview`)\\n- **CognitiveServicesAgentDeploymentResource** — agent deployments within a project (introduced in `2026-01-15-preview`)\\n- **CognitiveServicesConnectionResource** — connections associated with an account or project\\n- **CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostResource** — capability host management for an account or project\\n- **RaiPolicyResource** / **SubscriptionRaiPolicyResource** — Responsible AI content policies at the account or subscription scope\\n- **RaiBlocklistResource** / **RaiBlocklistItemResource** — custom blocklists for Responsible AI content filtering\\n- **RaiToolLabelResource** — RAI tool label management (introduced in `2026-01-15-preview`)\\n- **RaiTopicResource** — RAI topic management (introduced in `2026-01-15-preview`)\\n- **RaiExternalSafetyProviderSchemaResource** — external safety provider schema management (introduced in `2026-01-15-preview`)\\n\\nFor general Azure Resource Manager SDK concepts (paging, LRO, error handling) see the [Azure Resource Manager overview]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Add code snippets\\n\\nCreate a new sample test file and add snippet placeholders in the README.\\n\\n**File to create:** `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/tests/Samples/Sample1_ManageAgentApplications.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Core.TestFramework;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices.Models;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_ManageAgentApplications : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [AsyncOnly]\\n        public async Task CreateAgentApplication()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateCognitiveServicesAgentApplication\\n            // Get a reference to the subscription and resource group\\n            var armClient = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            var subscription = await armClient.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            var resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            // Get the Cognitive Services account\\n            var account = await resourceGroup.Value.GetCognitiveServicesAccountAsync(\\u0026#34;myAccount\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            // Get a project within the account\\n            var project = await account.Value.GetCognitiveServicesProjectAsync(\\u0026#34;myProject\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            // Create an agent application in the project\\n            var agentAppData = new CognitiveServicesAgentApplicationData\\n            {\\n                Properties = new CognitiveServicesAgentApplicationProperties\\n                {\\n                    // Set the required application properties here\\n                }\\n            };\\n            var operation = await project.Value.GetCognitiveServicesAgentApplications()\\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myAgentApp\\u0026#34;, agentAppData);\\n            CognitiveServicesAgentApplicationResource agentApp = operation.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created agent application: {agentApp.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Update README Examples section** — add snippet placeholder after the existing content:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create and manage a Cognitive Services Agent Application\\n\\n````C# Snippet:CreateCognitiveServicesAgentApplication\\n```\\n```\\n\\nCode samples for using the management library for .NET can be found in the following locations\\n- [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n````\\n\\n### Step 4: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/README.md` currently contains these application properties here\\n                }\\n            };\\n            var operation = await project.Value.GetCognitiveServicesAgentApplications()\\n                .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myAgentApp\\u0026#34;, agentAppData);\\n            CognitiveServicesAgentApplicationResource agentApp = operation.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created agent application: {agentApp.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**Update README Examples section** — add snippet placeholder after the existing content:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create and manage a Cognitive Services Agent Application\\n\\n````C# Snippet:CreateCognitiveServicesAgentApplication\\n```\\n```\\n\\nCode samples for using the management library for .NET can be found in the following locations\\n- [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n````\\n\\n### Step 4: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/README.md` currently contains these
